experiment: B22: B2 unchanged + mild recall-biased Tversky main mask loss
note: B22 keeps the B2 architecture, boundary/rim targets, auxiliary weights, and RBSG-lite unchanged. It only replaces the main mask Dice term with a Dice/Tversky mixture that penalizes false negatives slightly more than false positives. This directly targets the recall drop observed in B19, B20 and B21 without introducing another boundary/rim gate.

result_reflection.b18: EMA nearly tied B2 but shifted toward higher precision and lower recall
result_reflection.b19: flip consistency reduced both test Dice and recall
result_reflection.b20: inner distance supervision was too weak and core-biased; recall dropped
result_reflection.b21: IFE scale stayed at 0.0, so the new branch was effectively inactive
result_reflection.design_implication: use a small, interpretable loss-side correction for false negatives instead of more spatial modules

design_references.title: Focal Tversky Loss for Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: asymmetric Tversky weighting is useful for highly imbalanced medical foregrounds and small lesions

design_references.title: nnU-Net Revisited
design_references.adopted_idea: retain a strong U-Net recipe and validate conservative, isolated changes
